About OshiHub

Welcome to OshiNetwork - Est. 2022

## 🌐 What is OshiNetwork? You may be wondering what this service is, who runs it? what's the point?, what do we do? OshiNetwork is a passion project run by me, [Dalek](https://dalek.coffee)! I run it both for personal use and also extending the benefits to communities I most closely relate with and wish to support. OshiNetwork is NOT a business, and not for profit. **The goal is to cater to and support community!** ## 👤 Meet Dalek
Visit my website to learn more about me and my projects!
## 📜 Our History What began as **Baka.Social 🐘** in 2022 has evolved into a comprehensive network of services. Our journey reflects our commitment to providing community-focused alternatives to traditional platforms.
We launched as **Baka.Social**, a Mastodon server focused on creating a welcoming community-driven social platform
Migrated from Mastodon to Sharkey, rebranding as **Oshi.Social** to better serve our community with enhanced features and capabilities
Began offering additional services including **Carrd Pro** for personal websites, **AMP Panel** for game server hosting, and **Zernio (Formerly known as LATE)** for social media management
Services provided free of charge for the Oshi community

About Carrd Pro

Premium personal site hosting for OshiMembers

## Why Carrd Pro? You can already use Carrd for free without joining the Oshi tenant, but the free tier has some limitations. Joining us on the Oshi tenant gives you access to **Pro Standard features** including premium templates and custom domains! ## Value Comparison - Free tier limitations - No premium templates - subdomain.carrd.co only - Pro Standard costs $19/year minimum - Full Pro Standard features - All premium templates - Custom domain support - **FREE for OshiMembers** ## Features Available to OshiMembers Use your own domain with full SSL support via Let's Encrypt Access exclusive Pro templates not available on the free tier Add contact forms, signups, and embed widgets from services like Stripe, PayPal, and Typeform Remove the "Made with Carrd" attribution for a professional look ### Additional Pro Features - **Unlimited Elements**: No restrictions on elements per site - **High Quality Images**: Support for files up to 16MB (images/GIFs) and 64MB (videos) - **Custom Icons & Share Images**: Set custom site icons and social media share images - **Google Analytics**: Integrate analytics to track your site's performance - **Forms**: Add contact, signup, and other form types to collect information - **Embeds & Widgets**: Integrate third-party services seamlessly - **Priority Support**: Get help when you need it ## Getting Started Contact us through OshiSocial to request your Carrd Pro slot We'll add you to the Oshi tenant and send you an invitation link Start building with access to all Pro Standard features and templates Need help setting up a custom domain? We can assist with the configuration!
I can help with the domain setup! Just reach out once you have your site ready. 😊
## 🤝 Access & Terms ### Availability
Available to **OshiMembers** for free upon request with limited slots.
**Slots Available: 5/10** First come, first serve basis. If slots become full and new interested members request access, we will review and cleanup inactive slots (members inactive for over 6 months). ### ⛑️ Terms of Service & Privacy Policy
**Important**: This platform is NOT hosted on OshiNetwork services. Carrd is operated by their own company with their own policies.
Review Carrd's Terms of Service Review Carrd's Privacy Policy ## Need Help? Have questions about Carrd Pro or need assistance getting started? Feel free to reach out through OshiSocial!
Remember to check out the [Carrd documentation](https://carrd.co/docs) for detailed guides on using all the features available to you as a Pro member!

About OshiSocial

## 👋 Welcome to OshiSocial! 👋 OshiSocial is a fediverse instance created for the English speaking VR Creators who enjoy content creation utilizing platforms such as VRChat and Resonite.
Please be advised that while this community focuses on VR creators, it is not limited to it! **General posting is encouraged** so long as it abides by our [community guidelines](/oshisocial/community-guidelines) as well as US Law where our servers are hosted.
## 📃 Is OshiSocial Right for You? 📃 - You want to build genuine friendships with other creators. - You want a small, tight-knit creative community. - You are primarily an NSFW creator. ([Rule 3](/oshisocial/community-guidelines#3-nsfw-content-policy)) - Your main goal is farming engagement or follower counts. - You need an algorithm to promote your content. - You are located in or reside in an EU, EEA, or UK territory. ([Rule 8](/oshisocial/community-guidelines#8-regional-eligibility))
Please review [🤝 Joining OshiSocial 🤝](/oshisocial/joining) before signing up or we may reject your application!
## ❓ FAQ ❓ **YES!** — While OshiSocial is aimed at VR creators, the reason for this is NOT to limit what you post to VR content. Show us your morning coffee, pet pics, the gym session, whatever you want! The "VR Creator" audience is less about restricting the type of content, and more about building community/culture that understands each other 🥰. No worries! You can sign up on any other instance, and once you have gained some experience and confirmed content creation is for you, we can help you migrate over! OshiSocial allows you to move your account over and bring all your old followers, posts, media! After migration anyone who looks up your old account will also be redirected to your Oshi account! It is a tale old as time for fediverse instances to allow more people in than they can handle. Admins become overwhelmed, costs get out of hand, and they struggle to afford staying online. OshiSocial wants to take a responsible approach at staying online and reliable. Thanks to the fediverse, you can connect with friends and fans across any instance! We aim to foster connections among VR creators, artists, modelers, riggers, and other professionals. By prioritizing the core community of the instance for creators rather than a general-purpose platform, we believe we can greatly enhance the experience and maintain a viable model in the long term. Please check out our [🏦 Funding 🏦](/oshisocial/funding) page for more info!
Rules and guidelines for OshiSocial community members

OshiSocial Community Guidelines

## 📋 Overview OshiSocial focuses on VR creators but welcomes general posting that follows our rules and US law. We want everyone to feel comfortable here!
These guidelines are designed to maintain a safe, welcoming, and creative environment for all community members. Please read them carefully before participating.
## 📏 Community Rules ### 1️⃣ Age Requirement
🔞 **You must be 18+ to join this server.**
### 2️⃣ Community Behavior Be respectful to all members - we want everyone to feel comfortable here. Treat others as you would like to be treated. ### 3️⃣ NSFW Content Policy
OshiSocial is **NOT** an NSFW-focused platform. We maintain strict guidelines on adult content.
- Clean and wholesome content - Creative and artistic work - Community-friendly posts - Sensitive content must be legal in the US - Must be properly content warned/blurred/marked sensitive - Cannot comprise the majority of your activity - Content comprising most of a user's activity - IRL or self-posted explicit content - Self-harm, abuse against people/children/animals - Illegal content (under US law) - NSFW loli/shota (clothed VRC models are acceptable) ### 4️⃣ No Unsolicited Advances Respect personal boundaries. Do not send unwanted romantic or sexual messages to other members. ### 5️⃣ Bot Usage - Bots must be properly marked as bots - Bot activity must be non-spammy - Automated accounts must provide value to the community ### 6️⃣ Privacy & Safety **No doxxing or sharing private information** - This includes real names, addresses, phone numbers, or any other personal information without consent. ### 7️⃣ Legal Compliance No content that violates US law. This is non-negotiable and will result in immediate action. ### 8️⃣ Regional Eligibility
**OshiSocial does not accept registrations from individuals located in or residing in GDPR-covered territories**, including the European Union, European Economic Area, and United Kingdom. By creating an account, you confirm you are not located in or a resident of any GDPR-covered territory.
OshiSocial is a **free, non-commercial service** run out of the United States by a single individual with no revenue or funding. GDPR compliance for services offering registration to EU/EEA/UK residents requires appointing a formal EU representative - a legal obligation we simply cannot meet at this scale. Restricting registration to non-GDPR territories is the honest and responsible way to handle that limitation rather than ignoring it. If you're in one of these regions, we're sorry we can't accommodate you!
### 9️⃣ No Violence or Extremism - No incitement to violence - No promotion of violent ideologies - No threatening behavior ### 🔟 Political Content Political content requires appropriate content warnings. Keep discussions respectful and constructive. ### 1️⃣1️⃣ No Spam No spam accounts, excessive self-promotion, or repetitive content that degrades the user experience. ## ⚖️ Enforcement - Local feed: Proactively monitored - Global feed: Reactive monitoring - Community reports are reviewed promptly - Account freezing - Suspension - Account deletion - Staff discretion on grey areas If you believe an enforcement action was taken in error, you can appeal by contacting us at **help@oshi.social**. Please include: - Your username - The date of the action - Your reason for appeal - Any relevant context ## 🔄 Content Migration
If you're transitioning to creating more sexual content, we encourage you to check our [Migration Guides](/oshisocial/migration-guides) for help moving to servers better suited for NSFW content creators.
## 💭 Questions? If you have questions about these guidelines or need clarification on any rule, please don't hesitate to reach out to our moderation team at **help@oshi.social**.
These guidelines may be updated periodically. Major changes will be announced on the platform. Your continued use of OshiSocial constitutes acceptance of any updates.

OshiSocial Funding

OshiSocial will never require payment to join or use the platform.
**⚠️ Beware of scams!** OshiSocial will NEVER ask for payments through: - Direct Messages (DMs) - Email - Discord - Any other communication channel If anyone claims to represent OshiSocial and requests payment, it is a scam.
## 📊 Cost Breakdown 📊
*As of 12/19/2024*
**$25.18/yr** Annual domain registration and maintenance **$90/yr** Virtual Private Server infrastructure **$72/tb/yr** Media and data storage costs ## 💌 Contributions 💌 This is currently a passion project 100% funded by [@Dalekcoffee@oshi.social](https://oshi.social/@Dalekcoffee). **Why no donations?** Donations are not accepted at this time due to the tax complications that would arise from either: - Mixing donations with personal assets - Establishing a separate legal entity for the platform The current single-source funding model ensures simplicity and full control over the platform's direction. ## 🏷️ Advertising 🏷️ **Our Commitment:** We will never display advertisements that violate our Community Guidelines. **Our Goal:** Should we ever implement advertising, it will be used for fun and informational purposes that align with our community culture and values. **Future Advertising Vision** Any future advertising implementation would prioritize: - Community relevance - Entertainment value - Educational content - Strict adherence to our guidelines ## 🛟 Emergency Reserves 🛟 **Reserve Wallet:** bc1qc2hwjwnrvjnsyhsdupz5p69wq8wmg6wj03p9ep Since OshiSocial is currently 100% funded by a single individual, we maintain this Bitcoin wallet with sufficient reserves to provide **at least 3 months of advance warning** in the unlikely event that operations need to cease. **Purpose:** This wallet ensures operational continuity and responsible shutdown procedures if necessary. **Transparency:** Any withdrawals from this reserve will be announced within 24 hours.
**⚠️ This is NOT a donation wallet!** Please do NOT send any funds to the above address. Any funds sent will not be refunded as this wallet is strictly for emergency operational reserves.

OshiSocial DMCA, Reporting, & Moderation

## Reporting & Moderation OshiSocial moderates content from both local users and remote instances across the fediverse. We follow our [Community Guidelines](/oshisocial/community-guidelines) for all moderation decisions.
Please report posts directly within the application for the fastest response.
### Reporting Guidelines When reporting content, please keep in mind: When reporting accounts, include **at least a few posts that show rule-breaking behavior**, when applicable. Include additional context in your report comments. This is **especially important when the content is in a language nobody on the moderation team speaks**. ## DMCA Requests For DMCA takedown requests, please send your request to: **Email:** help@oshi.social
All DMCA requests must follow proper reporting procedure as per **17 U.S.C. 512(c)(3)**
We process all valid DMCA requests in accordance with United States copyright law and will respond as required by law.
Eligibility requirements and how to apply to join OshiSocial

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import CardGrid from '../../../components/CardGrid.astro'; import Card from '../../../components/Card.astro'; import Steps from '../../../components/Steps.astro'; import Step from '../../../components/Step.astro'; import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Joining OshiSocial

If you wish to join, please see below for eligibility and required info at signup.

**We encourage all prospective members to read our [🔨 Community Guidelines 🔨](/oshisocial/community-guidelines) before signing up.**
## Eligibility Requirements You need to be an active creator for at least 2 months. - We understand activity varies by the type of content you specialize in. Your content should align with our instance rules. - Generally, we do not accept NSFW creators. ## How to Apply
In order to verify your identity and prevent creator impersonation.
Please add your desired handle to your primary platform and place it somewhere obvious/easy to find such as your channel bio or a twitch panel. You can remove this after your account is approved! **(Example: @username@oshi.social)** Visit [https://oshi.social/](https://oshi.social/) and click on "Sign up at this instance" Please submit this info in the "Reason" for joining along with any message you want to pass to staff. **(Example: Hey it's me! the handle is in my bio @ https://twitch.tv/yourname)** Please allow up to 48 hours for approval. We will try to get to these ASAP, but we might not be immediately available.
Accounts that fail to submit the requested info under the reason for joining will be automatically rejected with or without notice. If you submitted the above info and have not heard back after 48hrs, feel free to contact us via email: help@oshi.social
The evolution and development history of OshiSocial platform

History of OshiSocial

## The Beginning: Baka.Social
In December 2022, Baka.Social was created as a 🐘 **Mastodon** instance for the VR Creator community.
### Original Logo (2022) The logo was a simplistic VRChat-themed design, which evolved into the "UwUBubble" design in 2023. ## Migration to Oshi.Social
In 2024, we migrated from Baka.Social to Oshi.Social and switched from Mastodon to 🦈 **Sharkey** software.
### Why We Migrated Better feature set in Sharkey for our community needs Improved performance and customization options Enhanced federation capabilities
Today, OshiSocial continues to serve the VR creator community as a free, open platform for creators to connect, share, and grow together.

OshiSocial How-to Guides

Complete tutorials and guides for mastering OshiSocial features

--- ## Fediverse Culture & Tips
**Post however you wish, it's your content!** Just remember to comply with our Community Guidelines. These tips will help enhance your fediverse presence and avoid common mistakes.
### ✅ Do's - Best Practices Consider using emoji to spice up your posts! They help beautify text posts and make them more engaging for your audience. Emoji can convey tone, add personality, and make your content stand out in timelines. Hashtags are essential for new users because **posts without hashtags are mostly only visible to your followers and local instance**. Many Mastodon and fediverse users discover content by following hashtags like topics, making them crucial for expanding your reach. **Best practices:** - Use relevant hashtags that match your content - Include 2-5 hashtags per post for optimal discovery - Check trending hashtags for current conversations - Create descriptive hashtags for niche topics - Hashtags can be placed in the body or after posts ### ❌ Don'ts - Common Mistakes to Avoid
The fediverse has different cultural norms than mainstream social media platforms. Respecting these differences is key to becoming a valued community member.
**This isn't Instagram!** Excessive or irrelevant hashtag use is considered spam and desperate behavior in the fediverse. Using unrelated hashtags just to gain visibility will very likely get you blocked and/or reported. **What to avoid:** - Stuffing posts with 10+ hashtags - Using popular but unrelated hashtags - Adding hashtags that don't match your content - Hashtag spam in replies or comments
Remember: People who follow hashtags see everything tagged with them. Irrelevant posts will anger topic followers and lead to blocks and reports.
While having backup accounts is understandable, posting identical content across multiple accounts creates duplicates in topic feeds. When users follow a hashtag, they'll see the same content multiple times, which is frustrating. **Consequences:** - You'll very likely be blocked and/or reported for spam - Damages your reputation across instances - Reduces engagement on all your accounts **Better approach:** - Share different aspects or perspectives on each account - Use boosts/reblogs to cross-promote - Download backups instead of creating duplicate accounts for archival - Ensure unique content across different accounts when posting similar topics ### ❕ Important Considerations
**Alt-Text for Images**: The fediverse is **VERY inclusive of the visually impaired**. While not technically mandatory, **it is considered rude to not include alt-text** from a cultural perspective.
Adding alt-text may be extra work, but it's a crucial accessibility feature that: - Helps screen reader users understand image content - Provides context when images fail to load - Improves searchability of your posts - Shows respect for all community members
**Quick Alt-Text Tips:** - Describe what's important in the image - Include text that appears in the image - Keep descriptions concise but informative - Mention colors, emotions, or actions if relevant - Consider the context and purpose of the image
We acknowledge that adding alt-text to every image may be "too much extra work" for some users, but making an effort when possible is greatly appreciated by the community.
### Additional Cultural Guidelines Use content warnings (CW) for: - Political discussions - Mental health topics - Food posts (for those with eating disorders) - Eye contact in photos - Animated GIFs (for those with epilepsy) - Spoilers for media - NSFW content - Number your threads (1/n, 2/n, etc.) - Reply to yourself to maintain thread structure - Use "thread" at the beginning for long posts - Don't break threads with unrelated replies - Consider using a blog for very long content - Boost (reblog) to share with your followers - Reply to add your thoughts - Quote posts may not be available on all instances - Don't boost old posts excessively - Consider the original poster's preferences --- ## ListenBrainz Integration Navigate to Settings → Integrations and locate the ListenBrainz section. Enter your ListenBrainz username to begin the connection process. Click "Authorize" to grant OshiSocial permission to display your listening history. You'll be redirected to ListenBrainz for authentication. Choose how your "Now Playing" music tile appears on your profile. You can customize the visibility and update frequency.
The "Now Playing" music tile will automatically update to show your current listening activity from any connected music service through ListenBrainz.
--- ## Advertisement Guidelines
Advertising on OshiSocial is meant to be used dominantly for fun and community engagement, not commercial purposes.
### Acceptable Advertising: - Creative projects and personal work - Community events and gatherings - Non-profit initiatives - Collaborative opportunities ### Not Permitted: - Spam or repetitive commercial posts - MLM or pyramid schemes - Misleading or deceptive content - Excessive self-promotion --- ## Discover Content & Follow Topics Use hashtags to discover content about specific topics. Popular hashtags appear in the trending section. Save hashtags and create custom feeds to stay updated on topics you care about. View the federated timeline to discover content from connected instances. Focus on your home instance's community by viewing the local timeline. --- ## Profile & Name Customization Your display name can include: - Emoji and special characters - Up to 30 characters - Unicode support for multiple languages To change: Settings → Profile → Display Name Your @username is permanent and cannot be changed after account creation. Choose carefully! Customize your profile with: - Bio text (up to 500 characters) - Custom profile fields (up to 4) - Links with verification support To edit: Settings → Profile → Edit Profile --- ## Theme Customization OshiSocial leverages Misskey/Sharkey's powerful customization capabilities: Choose from pre-installed themes: - Light and Dark modes - High contrast options - Seasonal themes - Community-created themes Customize your dashboard layout: - Rearrange widgets - Resize columns - Hide/show interface elements - Create multiple workspace layouts --- ## Preferences Backup Go to Settings → Data Export → Export Preferences. This creates a JSON file with all your customizations. Store the exported file in a safe location. Consider using cloud storage or version control. To restore: Settings → Data Import → Import Preferences. Upload your backup file to restore all settings. --- ## Push Notifications ### Enable Notifications: 1. Click the notification bell icon in the top bar 2. Select "Enable Push Notifications" 3. Grant browser permission when prompted 4. Customize which events trigger notifications ### Disable Notifications: 1. Access Settings → Notifications 2. Toggle off "Push Notifications" 3. Optionally revoke browser permissions
Push notifications require a modern browser and may not work in private/incognito mode.
--- ## Reporting Content OshiSocial strives to provide best-effort moderation reactively. Here's how to report issues:
Always review the [Community Guidelines](/oshisocial/community-guidelines) before reporting.
### Report Types: - **Spam**: Unsolicited commercial content or repetitive posts - **Harassment**: Targeted attacks or unwanted contact - **Inappropriate Content**: Violations of community standards - **Impersonation**: Accounts pretending to be someone else ### How to Report: 1. Click the three-dot menu on any post or profile 2. Select "Report" 3. Choose the appropriate category 4. Provide detailed context 5. Submit for moderator review --- ## Mute & Block Features **User Muting:** - Hides posts from specific users - They won't know they're muted - Can still see their profile if visited directly **Word Muting:** - Filter posts containing specific terms - Supports regular expressions - Works across all timelines **User Blocking:** - Prevents all interaction - Removes existing follows - Blocks mentions and DMs **Instance Blocking:** - Blocks entire instances - Useful for problematic servers - Admin-level blocks available View and manage all mutes/blocks: - Settings → Mutes & Blocks - Easy unmute/unblock options - Import/export block lists - Temporary mute durations --- ## Additional Resources Read our complete community standards and expectations Important information about protecting your privacy Moving from another instance or platform Current platform status and known issues
These tutorials are regularly updated. For the latest information, check our [status updates](/oshisocial/status-updates) page.

OshiSocial Migration Guides

Simple guides to help you navigate between platforms and make the most of OshiSocial!

## Platform Comparison ### Sharkey vs Mastodon Features - **React to post with Emoji** - Go beyond simple Likes with multiple reaction types - **Built-in drive system** - Manage and organize your media files efficiently - **Enhanced customization** - More UI options and themes available - **Better threading display** - Improved conversation visualization - **Post import support** - Can import posts and media when migrating - **Wider app ecosystem** - More third-party apps available - **Simpler interface** - Easier for beginners to navigate - **Broader instance selection** - More servers to choose from - **More established platform** - Larger overall user base ### What Transfers Between Platforms **Always Transferable:** - ✅ Followers - ✅ Following lists - ✅ Bookmarks - ✅ Blocks and mutes **Platform-Specific Transfer Capabilities:** - **Mastodon → Sharkey:** ✅ Posts and media CAN be transferred - **Sharkey → Mastodon:** ❌ Posts and media CANNOT be transferred (Mastodon limitation)
When migrating, your old account will become a redirect-only account and will no longer be fully functional. Plan your migration carefully!
## Migration Guides ## Migrating from Mastodon to Sharkey ### Pre-Migration Steps #### Step 1: Create Your User Archive On your Mastodon instance, go to **Preferences → Import and Export** Click **"Request your archive"** to create a full backup Wait up to 24 hours for an email notification that your archive is ready Download the ZIP file containing your posts and media #### Step 2: Export Additional Data (Optional) From the same Import and Export page, download CSV files for: Follows, Bookmarks, Blocks, Mutes ### Migration Process #### Phase 1: Setup New Sharkey Account Create your new account on the target Sharkey server (e.g., oshi.social) Navigate to **Settings → Account Migration** Enter your old Mastodon handle (format: `@oldusername@example.com`) Click **Save** to establish the link #### Phase 2: Initiate Migration from Mastodon Log back into your OLD Mastodon account Go to **Preferences → Account → "Move to a different account"** Enter your NEW Sharkey handle (format: `@newusername@sharkey.server`) Enter your password and click **"Move followers"**
After this step, your old Mastodon account becomes redirect-only and followers will be moved to your new account.
### Post-Migration: Import Your Data On your NEW Sharkey account, go to **Settings → Import and export** Upload your files: Archive ZIP for posts and media, CSV files for follows, bookmarks, blocks, and mutes Watch for the upload indicator in the top right corner Wait for the "import in progress" message. Imports can take many hours depending on data size.
**Important Security Note:** After import completes, DELETE the uploaded ZIP files from your Sharkey drive as they are publicly accessible!
If an import fails, wait at least 24 hours before attempting again and contact an admin to avoid creating duplicate data.
## Migrating from Sharkey to Mastodon
Due to Mastodon software limitations, posts and media CANNOT be transferred when migrating from Sharkey to Mastodon. Only followers, follows, bookmarks, blocks, and mutes can be migrated.
### Pre-Migration: Export Your Data On your Sharkey server, navigate to **Settings** (right sidebar) Select **"Import and Export"** from the settings menu Export: Follows list, Bookmarks, Blocks list, Mutes list Your exported files will be stored in your Sharkey drive for download ### Migration Process #### Phase 1: Setup Mastodon Account Create your new account on the destination Mastodon server Go to **Preferences → Account** Under "Moving from a different account," select **"Create an account alias"** Enter your old Sharkey username (format: `@oldusername@sharkey.server`) Click **"Create alias"** to establish the connection #### Phase 2: Initiate Migration from Sharkey Log back into your OLD Sharkey account Navigate to **Settings → Account Migration → "Migrate this account to a different one"** Enter your NEW Mastodon username and password Click **Migrate** to transfer your followers
Your old Sharkey account will no longer be fully functional after migration. It will become a redirect-only account.
### Post-Migration: Import Additional Data Log into your NEW Mastodon account and go to **Preferences → "Import and export" → Import** Upload the CSV files you exported from Sharkey: Follows list, Bookmarks, Blocks list, Mutes list Wait for each import to complete before starting the next one
Remember that your posts and media from Sharkey cannot be imported to Mastodon. Consider downloading your archive from Sharkey as a personal backup before migrating.
## Best Practices for Migration ### Before You Migrate - **Announce your move** - Let your followers know you're migrating in advance - **Backup everything** - Download all your data even if it can't be imported - **Update your bio** - Include your new handle in your old account's bio before migrating - **Choose timing wisely** - Migrate during low-activity periods to minimize disruption ### After Migration - **Verify imports** - Check that your follows, blocks, and other data transferred correctly - **Update external links** - Change any websites or profiles that link to your old account - **Re-introduce yourself** - Make an introduction post on your new account - **Be patient** - It may take time for all followers to migrate and for federation to stabilize
Need help with your migration? Contact the OshiSocial admin team for assistance!
Set up the 'now playing' music tile feature on your OshiSocial profile using ListenBrainz

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import CardGrid from '../../../components/CardGrid.astro'; import Card from '../../../components/Card.astro'; import Steps from '../../../components/Steps.astro'; import Step from '../../../components/Step.astro'; import Accordion from '../../../components/Accordion.astro'; import AccordionGroup from '../../../components/AccordionGroup.astro'; import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components'; # ListenBrainz Integration Display your currently playing music on your OshiSocial profile with ListenBrainz integration. This federated feature shows what you're listening to in real-time. ## What is ListenBrainz? ListenBrainz is an open-source music listening tracker that integrates with OshiSocial (powered by Sharkey fediverse software) to display your "now playing" music on your profile. This creates a dynamic music tile that updates automatically as you listen.
This feature is federated but requires compatible fediverse software to display properly on other instances.
## Quick Setup Guide Visit [listenbrainz.org](https://listenbrainz.org) and click "Create Account"
Remember your username! You'll need it to connect ListenBrainz to OshiSocial.
Navigate to **Settings → Profile** on OshiSocial and place your username here. Enter your ListenBrainz username from Step 1 in the designated field Choose one of the integration methods below based on your preferred music platform ## Music Service Integrations Choose the integration method that matches how you listen to music: ### Spotify Integration (Recommended) The easiest and most reliable integration for Spotify users. Go to your [ListenBrainz Music Service settings](https://listenbrainz.org/settings/music-services/) Select **"Record listening history"** for the best experience
This option provides the most complete integration with real-time updates
Begin playing music on Spotify. It may take a few songs before the tile appears on your profile. ### Web Scrobbler (Universal) Works with YouTube, SoundCloud, Spotify Web, Apple Music Web, and more.
Always review browser extension permissions before installing. The Web Scrobbler is open-source and you can review its code on GitHub.
Download from the [Web Scrobbler GitHub page](https://github.com/web-scrobbler/web-scrobbler) After installation, configure which music services to track When prompted, authenticate with your ListenBrainz account ### Apple Music Integration Requires an active Apple Music subscription.
Apple Music integration requires re-authentication every 6 months due to Apple's security policies.
Visit your ListenBrainz Music Service settings Follow the Apple Music connection prompts Authorize ListenBrainz to access your Apple Music listening data ### Alternative Integration Methods Use the built-in player on ListenBrainz website. Interface differs from mainstream platforms but works without additional setup. Advanced users can integrate custom music sources using the ListenBrainz API documentation.
**SoundCloud Integration**: Currently broken as of August 2024. Check ListenBrainz updates for restoration status.
## Troubleshooting - Verify your ListenBrainz username is correctly entered in OshiSocial settings - Check that your music service is actively sending data to ListenBrainz - Some tracks may take 2-3 songs to start displaying - Ensure your profile visibility settings allow the music tile to show - Re-authenticate your music service on ListenBrainz - For Apple Music: Re-authentication required every 6 months - Check if browser extensions need updating - Verify ListenBrainz service status The music tile feature requires compatible fediverse software. Users on instances running Sharkey, Misskey, or compatible forks will see your music. Mastodon and some other platforms may not display this feature. ## Tips for Best Experience In most cases, only one integration method is needed unless you use multiple music platforms regularly. Your listening history becomes public. Consider what you're comfortable sharing before enabling. This feature works best within Sharkey/Misskey-compatible instances. The music tile updates in near real-time without affecting your profile's loading speed. ## Platform Compatibility | Platform | Integration Method | Reliability | |----------|-------------------|-------------| | Spotify | Direct Integration | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent | | YouTube | Web Scrobbler | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Very Good | | Apple Music | Direct or Extension | ⭐⭐⭐ Good (needs re-auth) | | SoundCloud | Currently Broken | ❌ Unavailable | | Local Files | Developer API | ⭐⭐ Requires setup |
This feature enhances your OshiSocial profile with dynamic music presence, making your profile more engaging and personal while connecting with others through shared musical interests.
OshiSocial Privacy Policy and data handling practices

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

OshiSocial Privacy Policy

**Last Updated: 20260212**
This privacy policy describes how OshiSocial ("Oshi.social", "we", "us") collects, protects, and uses the personally identifiable information you may provide through the OshiSocial website or its API. ## Contact Information - **Name:** Oshi.social - **Representative:** Dalek - **Email:** help@oshi.social ## What information do we collect? **Basic account information:** If you register on this server, you may be asked to enter a username, an e-mail address, and a password. You may also enter additional profile information such as a display name and biography, and upload a profile picture and header image. The username, display name, biography, profile picture, and header image are always listed publicly. **Posts, following, and other public information:** The list of people you follow is listed publicly; the same is true for your followers. When you submit a message, the date and time are stored as well as the application you submitted the message from. Public and unlisted posts are available publicly. Your posts are delivered to your followers; in some cases, it means they are delivered to different servers, and copies are stored there. **Compression and Metadata Removal:** When you upload media and choose to compress it, we automatically remove any embedded metadata (EXIF data) to protect your privacy. However, if you choose not to compress your media, the original EXIF metadata may be preserved and could potentially be accessible to others who view your media.
Certain devices such as smartphones, drones, action cameras and more with built in GPS modules may embed this information on your images. We recommend you compress images before uploading them to ensure any sensitive metadata (like location data) is removed.
**Direct and followers-only posts:** All posts are stored and processed on the server. Followers-only posts are delivered to your followers and users who are mentioned in them, and direct posts are delivered only to users mentioned in them. Please keep in mind that the operators of the server and any receiving server may view such messages, and that recipients may screenshot, copy, or otherwise reshare them. Do not share any sensitive information over OshiSocial. **IPs and other metadata:** When you log in, we record the IP address you log in from, as well as the name of your browser application. The latest IP address used is stored for up to 12 months. ## What do we use your information for? Any of the information we collect from you may be used in the following ways: - To provide the core functionality of OshiSocial. - To aid moderation of the community, for example comparing your IP address with other known ones to determine ban evasion or other violations, and to hash images for scanning, identifying, and reporting of CSAM material. - The email address you provide may be used to send you information, notifications about other people interacting with your content or sending you messages, and to respond to inquiries. ## How do we protect your information? We implement a variety of security measures to maintain the safety of your personal information. Among other things, your browser session, as well as the traffic between your applications and the API, are secured with SSL, and your password is hashed using a strong one-way algorithm. You may enable two-factor authentication to further secure access to your account. ## What is our data retention policy? We will make a good faith effort to: - Retain server logs containing the IP address of all requests to this server no more than 90 days. - Retain the IP addresses associated with registered users no more than 12 months. You can request and download an archive of your content, including your posts, media attachments, profile picture, and header image. You may irreversibly delete your account at any time. ## Do we use cookies? Yes. Cookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer's hard drive through your Web browser. These cookies enable the site to recognize your browser and, if you have a registered account, associate it with your registered account. We use cookies to understand and save your preferences for future visits. ## Do we disclose any information to outside parties? We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your personally identifiable information. We may also release your information when we believe release is appropriate to comply with the law, enforce our site policies, or protect ours or others rights, property, or safety. Your public content may be downloaded by other servers in the fediverse network. ## Site usage by children Our site, products, and services are all directed to people who are at least 18 years old. If you are under the age of 18, per the direction of the admin team, do not use this site.
Important privacy and safety information for OshiSocial users

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import CardGrid from '../../../components/CardGrid.astro'; import Card from '../../../components/Card.astro'; import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

OshiSocial Privacy Safety Reminder

## The Fediverse Isn't Private, and That's OK! Welcome to OshiSocial! We're thrilled to have you join our community where you can share art, posts, ideas, and connect with others. However, it's crucial to understand that this is a **public social platform**, not a place meant for private or secure conversations. Everything you post on the Fediverse should be considered public by default. Treat your interactions here as if you were speaking in a public square – visible to anyone who wants to listen.
Remember: Your safety and privacy are important. Always err on the side of caution when sharing information online.
## Direct Messages Are Not Secure
While OshiSocial doesn't monitor your Direct Messages (DMs), the federated nature of our platform means other servers could technically see or store messages that pass through them.
For truly private conversations, we strongly recommend using encrypted messaging platforms: - **Telegram** (Secret Chats feature) - **Discord** (Private servers or DMs) - **Signal** or other encrypted messengers - **Encrypted email services** These platforms are specifically designed with privacy and security in mind, unlike the inherently public nature of federated social networks. ## File Upload Caution
**EXIF DATA WARNING:** Certain devices such as smartphones, drones, action cameras and more with built-in GPS modules may embed this information on your images. We recommend you compress images before uploading them to ensure any sensitive metadata (like location data) is removed.
When you upload files to OshiSocial, they become accessible via a public URL. This means: - Photos and videos can be viewed by anyone who has the link - Documents and other files are similarly accessible - There's no way to fully restrict access once a file is uploaded ### Secure File Sharing Alternatives For private file sharing, consider using: - **Google Drive** (with proper permission settings) - **Proton Drive** (end-to-end encrypted) - **Dropbox** (with adjusted privacy settings) - **Discord DMs** (for smaller files) ## Key Takeaways The Fediverse is **public by design**. This makes it excellent for: - Community interaction - Sharing creative work - Public discussions - Building connections But remember to: - Treat it like a public space - Use privacy-focused apps for sensitive content - Never share personal information publicly - Be mindful of metadata in your uploads
Think before you post: Would you be comfortable with this information being permanently public?
## Additional Resources Review our complete terms of service Learn about how we handle your data ## Need Help? If you have any questions or concerns about privacy and safety on OshiSocial, please don't hesitate to contact us: 📧 **Email:** help@oshi.social
OshiSocial Terms of Service and usage agreement

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

OshiSocial Terms of Service

**Last Updated: 20260212**
These terms and conditions ("Terms") govern your use of OshiSocial and its associated offerings ("Services"). By accessing or using the Services, you agree to be bound by these Terms. Please read these Terms carefully before using OshiSocial. **1. Agreement to Terms** You may use the Services only if you agree to form a binding contract with us and are not a person barred from receiving services under the laws of the applicable jurisdiction. In any case, you must be at least 18 years old to use the Services. If you are accepting these Terms and using the Services on behalf of a company, organization, government, or other legal entity, you represent and warrant that you are authorized to do so and have the authority to bind such entity to these Terms, in which case the words "you" and "your" as used in these Terms shall refer to such entity. **2. Service Availability** OshiSocial is offered in "good faith" and on a "best effort" basis. We strive to provide a reliable service, but we cannot guarantee: * Flawless service: There may be occasional bugs or downtime. * Redundancy: Our hosting platform currently lacks failover servers. If the server goes down, there will be an outage. * Perfect updates: We push code changes manually in production, and mistakes can happen. However, we do have some positives: * Lightweight service: This translates to faster performance. * Regular backups: We have host-level backups in place to minimize data loss. * Uptime record: We have a strong uptime history of over 90% with no known security breaches (as of 2/12/2026). **3. Disclaimer of Warranties** OshiSocial is provided "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE." OshiSocial disclaims all warranties and conditions, whether express or implied, including but not limited to the warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. **4. User Responsibility and Content** You are responsible for your use of the Services and for any Content you provide, including compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations. You should only provide Content that you are comfortable sharing with others. By using OshiSocial, you agree that we may process your content, information, and interactions to Third Party Services as necessary to integrate with the activitypub protocol. Please be aware that you are directing us to deliver your information to services not controlled by OshiSocial, so that your information can be stored and processed by the Third Party Services. You also agree not to misuse the Services, for example, by interfering with them or accessing them using a method other than the interface and the instructions that we provide. You agree that you will not work around any technical limitations in the software provided to you as part of the Services. **5. Privacy disclaimer for Direct Messages and Drive storage** Please be aware that drive uploads or direct messages sent through ANY fediverse server (including but not limited to OshiSocial) are currently not encrypted. This means that your messages may be accessible to the server admins. OshiSocial does NOT monitor Direct messages. We cannot guarantee other server admins on the receiving end do not either. (This is applicable when sending direct messages to people on other servers). Nevertheless, we HIGHLY recommend that you use other platforms for private/sensitive communication or file sharing. Direct messages should be used for more discreet tagging, but NOT as an encrypted privacy tool. **6. Limitation of Liability** To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, OshiSocial shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits or revenues, whether incurred directly or indirectly, or any loss of data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses, resulting from your access to or use of or inability to access or use the Services, or any Content obtained from the Services. **7. Termination** We reserve the right to terminate your access to OshiSocial at any time, with or without notice, for any reason, including a violation of these Terms. **8. Changes to the Terms** We may change these Terms at any time. We will post the updated Terms on our documentation website with notice via our fediverse server. Your continued use of OshiSocial after the posting of any revised Terms constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms. **9. Contact Us** If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at help@oshi.social

🌌 Federation 🌌

Oshi.social has blocked communication or certain aspects with the following servers for various reasons which include but are not limited to: - Servers that do not align with our Community Guidelines - Servers that have no active or functioning moderation - Servers that have spam - Servers that have shut down ## Server Moderation List
This list shows instances that OshiSocial has restricted or blocked. Actions taken: - **Blocked**: Not Reachable from Oshi.social - **Silenced**: Hidden unless you follow first - **NSFW**: Content automatically marked as NSFW
| Instance | Action | Reason | |----------|--------|--------| | bsky.brid.gy | NSFW | Some content does not align with our server guidelines. But it is not the whole instance culture. | | M\*\*\*key.io | Instance and media silenced | Some content does not align with our server guidelines. But it is not the whole instance culture. | | p\*\*\*o.net | Silenced & NSFW | Some content does not align with our server guidelines. But it is not the whole instance culture. | | shit\*\*\*ter.club | Blocked | Instance shutdown, moved to shitposter.world but the old domain was causing backlog in our queue. | | social.bo\*\*\*sser.systems | Blocked | Instance shutdown, moved to s.boykisser.systems but the old domain was causing backlog in our queue. | | l\*\*\*y.world | Blocked | Causing TONS of database errors due to invalid notes sent | | cut\*\*\*t\*\*rls.cafe | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | im\*\*\*o.exposed | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | 4\*\*\*key.net | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | r\*\*e.pet | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | b\*\*\*ag.net | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | det\*\*\*\*\*\*otcity.com | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | pos\*\*\*\*\*\*\*licon.rocks | Blocked | Does not align with our server guidelines. | | a\*.\*iki | Blocked | Lots of spam from automated bots. | | rss-mstdn.s\*\*\*iofr\*\*\*ia.com | Blocked | Lots of spam from automated bots. | | lepou\*\*\*\*monde.com | Silenced | Majority of users are bots with tons of bot spam. |
Please be aware that some server names contain very malicious content, profanity and/or hate speech. We do NOT recommend our community members visit these sites as they also may contain very sensitive and/or illegal content. This list is provided with the intent of transparency in moderation.
## Appeal Process If you believe a server has been incorrectly blocked, you can appeal the decision through our [Google Forms appeal process](https://forms.google.com). ## Cross-Reference You can verify this list against the [OshiSocial Federation information](https://oshi.social) for real-time federation status.
This transparency page is focused on providing clear information about our federation decisions to prioritize community safety and comfort when engaging with other fediverse servers.

About AMP Panel

AMP Panel is a free game server hosting service provided to the VR creator community. ## Service Overview AMP Panel offers hosting for: - **Resonite Headless Servers** - Host your own Resonite sessions - **Game Servers** - Various game server hosting options - **File Sharing** - OshiShare integration for file distribution ## Features ### Free Hosting - No cost for basic server hosting - Community-supported infrastructure - Designed for VR creators and their communities ### Easy Management - Web-based control panel - One-click server deployment - Automated backups (best-effort) - Simple configuration options ### Resonite Integration - Specialized support for Resonite headless servers - Repair tools for common issues - Session management features - World hosting capabilities ## Getting Started 1. **Sign Up** - Create your account at AMP Panel 2. **Select Server Type** - Choose from available server options 3. **Configure** - Set up your server parameters 4. **Deploy** - Launch your server with one click 5. **Manage** - Use the web panel for ongoing management ## Limitations - Resources are limited based on availability - Best-effort service with no guaranteed uptime - Users responsible for their own backups - Subject to Terms of Service ## Support For technical support: - Check the [Guides](/oshipanel/guides) section - Visit the status page at hub.oshi.social/#status - Contact: help@oshi.social AMP Panel is another free service provided by the Oshi Network to support the VR creator community.

This page will be updated with detailed instructions soon. ### Coming Soon: - Step-by-step backup creation guide - Export procedures - Restoration instructions - Backup scheduling options - Storage location details - Retention policies ## Current Backup Features While documentation is pending, AMP Panel currently offers: - Manual backup creation - Backup downloads - Basic restoration options ## Best Practices Until full documentation is available: 1. **Create regular backups** - Don't wait for automatic systems 2. **Download your backups** - Store copies locally 3. **Test restores** - Verify your backups work 4. **Multiple copies** - Don't rely on a single backup ## Your Responsibility Remember: - AMP Panel backups are best-effort - You are responsible for your data - Always maintain external backups - Don't assume backups are automatic ## Support For urgent backup assistance: - Email: help@oshi.social - Include your server details - Describe what you need to backup Full documentation will be available soon. About Zernio

## 🎉 Introducing Zernio 🎉
Link available on the network page! [Visit OshiHub](https://hub.oshi.social/)
A social media management platform designed to help you schedule and organize your social content all in one place!
**Premium Feature**: Upload your media once - Zernio automatically optimizes it for all platforms you post to! No need to create platform-specific versions. 🎉
## Supported Platforms
**Twitter/X Support**: You can use your own API key (BYOK) or request access to the communal key covered by Dalek. See the [Twitter/X API Setup](/zernio/twitter-api) page for full details.
Unfortunately, Zernio does not currently support OshiSocial specifically. This has been requested - if you want to support this feature request, please upvote and comment on the [Fediverse Platform Integrations](https://zernio.featurebase.app/p/fediverse-platform-integrations) feedback request! (Misskey API compatibility would enable OshiSocial support, as we run a fork of Misskey)
## 🤝 Access & Terms ### Availability
Available to **OshiMembers** for free upon request with limited slots.
**Slots Available: 41/50** First come, first serve basis. If slots become full and new interested members request access, we will review and cleanup inactive slots (members inactive for over 6 months). ### ⛑️ Terms of Service & Privacy Policy
**Important**: This platform is NOT hosted on OshiNetwork services. Zernio is operated by their own company with their own policies.

How to safely disconnect Zernio from your social media accounts

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import CardGrid from '../../../components/CardGrid.astro'; import Card from '../../../components/Card.astro'; import Steps from '../../../components/Steps.astro'; import Step from '../../../components/Step.astro'; import Accordion from '../../../components/Accordion.astro'; import AccordionGroup from '../../../components/AccordionGroup.astro'; import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Revoking Zernio Permissions

How to safely disconnect Zernio from your connected accounts

## 🔐 Why You Might Need This If you're done using Zernio, switching to a different scheduling tool, or just want to clean up your connected apps, you'll need to revoke Zernio's permissions on each platform you connected.
**Revoking access is permanent.** You'll need to reconnect and re-authorize Zernio if you want to use it again later.
Removing Zernio's access stops it from posting on your behalf or reading your account data. However, **it doesn't delete posts Zernio already made** - those stay on your timeline unless you manually delete them.
## 📱 Platform-Specific Instructions --- ### 🎵 TikTok
**TikTok app permission management is mobile only.** There is no equivalent setting available on the TikTok website.
Tap your **Profile** icon in the bottom-right corner of the TikTok app. Tap the **three-line menu** (hamburger icon) in the top-right corner. Select **Settings and Privacy** → **Account** → **Security and Permissions**. Tap **Apps and Services Permissions** to see all connected apps. Find **Zernio** (may also appear as **Social Media Connector**) in the list, tap it, then tap **Remove Access** and confirm.
After removal, Zernio can no longer post videos or access your TikTok account info.
--- ### 📷 Instagram Go to [instagram.com/accounts/manage_access/](https://www.instagram.com/accounts/manage_access/) in your browser. Find **Zernio** (may also appear as **Social Media Connector**) in the list, click **Remove**, and confirm when prompted. Tap your **profile icon** in the bottom-right corner. Tap **App and Website Permissions** directly. Find **Zernio** in the list of connected apps, tap **Remove** next to it, and confirm. Tap your **profile icon**, tap the **three-line menu**, then select **Settings and Privacy**. Tap **Account Center** → **Apps and Websites**. Look under the **Active** tab, find **Zernio**, tap **Remove** and confirm. --- ### 🧵 Threads Go to [threads.com/settings/website_permissions](https://www.threads.com/settings/website_permissions) in your browser. Find **Zernio** (may also appear as **Social Media Connector**) in the list and remove it. Tap your **profile icon** in the bottom-right corner of Threads. Tap the menu → **Account** → **Website Permissions**. Find **Zernio** in the list, tap it, and remove it. --- ### 📘 Facebook Click your **profile icon** → **Settings & Privacy** → **Settings**. Click **Apps and Websites** in the left sidebar. Find **Zernio** in the list, click it, then click the **Remove** button.
Facebook offers an option to **delete posts made by the app** when you remove access. If you want to keep your Zernio-posted content, make sure this option is **unchecked**.
Tap the **three-line menu** in the bottom-right (iOS) or top-right (Android). Tap **Settings & Privacy** → **Settings** → **Apps and Websites**. Find **Zernio** in the list of connected apps, tap it, scroll down, and select **Remove**.
Facebook offers an option to **delete posts made by the app** when you remove access. If you want to keep your Zernio-posted content, make sure this option is **unchecked**.
--- ### 𝕏 X (Twitter)
X shows you exactly what permissions each app has (like "Read and Write" or "Direct Message Access"). Check these before revoking if you're unsure which app is which.
Go to [x.com/settings/connected_apps](https://x.com/settings/connected_apps) in your browser. Find **Zernio** (may also appear as **Social Media Connector**, **OshiPost**, or **OshiZernio**) in the list and use **Revoke Access** to remove each one. Tap your **profile icon** in the top-left corner. Select **Settings and Privacy** → **Security and Account Access** → **Apps and Sessions** → **Connected Apps**. Find **Zernio**, tap it, then tap **Revoke Access** and confirm. --- ### 📌 Pinterest
**Pinterest permission management must be done via the web.** The mobile app does not support revoking third-party app access.
Go to [pinterest.com/settings/security/](https://www.pinterest.com/settings/security/) in your browser. Find **Zernio** in the list of connected apps, click **Delete** next to it, and confirm. --- ### 🤖 Reddit
**Reddit permission management must be done via the web.** There is no equivalent option in the Reddit mobile app.
Go to [reddit.com/prefs/apps](https://www.reddit.com/prefs/apps) in your browser. Find **Zernio** (may also appear as **LATE** or **Social Media Connector**) in the list and click **revoke access**. --- ### 🔵 Bluesky Go to [bsky.app/settings/app-passwords](https://bsky.app/settings/app-passwords) in your browser. Find the password you created for Zernio, then click the **trash can** or **Delete** icon. In the Bluesky app, tap **Settings** → **Privacy and Security** → **App Passwords**. Find the password you created for Zernio, then tap the **Delete** icon. --- ### 🎮 Discord
**Zernio does NOT connect to your personal Discord account.** Instead, it installs a **bot** on your Discord server. To revoke access, you need to kick the bot from your server.
Only server admins or users with **Manage Server** permissions can remove bots.
Click your **server name** at the top of the channel list and select **Server Settings**. Navigate to **Integrations** in the left sidebar. Find **Zernio** in your list of bots. Click it, then select **Remove Integration** or **Kick** to revoke its access. --- ### 📺 YouTube Go to [myaccount.google.com/connections](https://myaccount.google.com/connections) in your browser. Search for **Zernio** or **Social Media Connector**, click the result, then click **Delete all connections** and confirm.
If you're a YouTube creator, also check the **Permissions** tab in **YouTube Studio** to make sure no one was granted Manager or Editor roles through Zernio.
Tap your **profile picture** in the YouTube app, then tap **Accounts** → **Manage your Google Account**. Tap **Third-party apps & services**. Find **Zernio**, tap it, then tap **Revoke access** and confirm. ## ❓ Common Questions **No.** Once you revoke Zernio's permissions, it can no longer post to that platform. Any scheduled posts in your Zernio queue **will not publish** for that account. **No.** Content that Zernio already posted to your account stays there. You'll need to manually delete those posts if you want them removed. The exception is **Facebook**, which gives you the option to delete app-posted content when you revoke access. Revoking access prevents Zernio from making **future** requests for your data, but it doesn't automatically delete data they already have on their servers. If you want your data removed from Zernio's systems, you should **contact Zernio support directly** and request deletion under privacy laws like GDPR or CCPA. **Yes!** Revoking access isn't permanent in the sense that you can always re-authorize Zernio by going through the connection process again. You'll just need to grant permissions from scratch. **Yes.** Each platform manages permissions independently, so you'll need to follow the steps for each social account you connected to Zernio. There's no "revoke all" button that works across platforms. Either you never connected that platform to Zernio, or the connection was already removed. You're good to go for that platform. ## 🛡️ After You Revoke - Zernio can no longer post to your accounts - Zernio can't read your profile data or posts - Scheduled posts will not publish - You can reconnect anytime by re-authorizing - Past posts are **not** automatically deleted - Data Zernio already collected is **not** automatically deleted - Your Zernio account itself is **not** deleted (you need to do that separately if wanted)
If you want to **fully delete your Zernio account** (not just disconnect platforms), you'll need to do that from within Zernio's settings. That's a separate process from revoking platform permissions.
## 📧 Still Have Questions? If you run into issues or have questions about using Zernio with OshiNetwork services, reach out to the community: **Email:** help@oshi.social
How to configure Twitter/X API credentials for Zernio social media scheduling

import Frame from '../../../components/Frame.astro'; import Card from '../../../components/Card.astro'; import Steps from '../../../components/Steps.astro'; import Step from '../../../components/Step.astro'; import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

X/Twitter API Setup for Zernio

## 🔑 API Key Options To use Zernio with Twitter/X, you have two options for API credentials: use the communal API key provided by Dalek, or set up your own personal API key.
**Twitter/X has discontinued their free tier API.** All API access now operates on a pay-as-you-go model. This affects both the communal key and any personal keys you set up yourself.
The simplest way to get started is to use Zernio's communal API key. Dalek is currently covering this cost so it remains free for community members — no developer account needed.
**Heads up about the future of the communal key.** Because Twitter/X no longer offers a free tier, the communal key has ongoing costs. Dalek is happy to cover it for now, but if usage grows to the point where costs become unsustainable, we may need to discontinue it. If that happens, you'll have two paths forward: - **Bring Your Own Key (BYOK)** - set up your own personal Twitter API key - **Managed Key Service** - Dalek may offer a paid managed key option for those who prefer not to deal with setup If anything changes, Dalek will communicate well in advance so no one gets caught off guard.
Reach out to Dalek through [OshiHub](https://hub.oshi.social/) and request access to the communal API key. Dalek will help configure your account with the communal credentials. You're ready to start scheduling your posts!
**Benefits of using the communal key:** - No developer account needed - No configuration required - Free while Dalek covers the cost - Perfect for casual users
For users who want full control over their API access, you can set up your own Twitter/X developer credentials under a pay-as-you-go plan.
**Benefits of using your own key:** - Full control over your API access and usage - Independent from shared resources - You manage your own rate limits and spend - Required for high-volume usage
**Security Warning**: Never share your API credentials with anyone except trusted support staff. These credentials provide full access to your Twitter account.
## Setting Up Your Twitter/X API Credentials Navigate to the [Twitter Developer Portal](https://developer.twitter.com/) and sign in with your Twitter/X account. If you don't already have one, you'll need to apply for a developer account. Fill out the use case form when prompted.
💡 **If asked to describe your Twitter API use cases (250 characters), copy this text:** > Our application enables users to schedule and publish text and media content to their own Twitter accounts using their personal API credentials. We access user profile data, publish tweets with media attachments, retrieve engagement metrics, and manage authentication.
From your developer dashboard, create a new **Project**, then create an **App** inside it. Give it a name you'll recognize (e.g., "Zernio Scheduler"). Before you do anything else, go to your **Billing & Usage** settings and configure a monthly spend cap.
**Set a spend limit — seriously.** Twitter/X operates on pay-as-you-go pricing with no free tier. Without a cap, unexpected usage spikes can result in charges you didn't plan for. Set a limit you're comfortable with before connecting your key to any app.
In your App settings, set the following permissions: - **Read and Write** (for posting tweets) - **Read Users** (for profile access) Make sure you're on at least the **Basic** tier — this is required for write access (posting tweets). Go to the **Keys and Tokens** tab in your App settings. Generate and copy the following: - **API Key** (Client ID) - **API Key Secret** (Client Secret) Store these somewhere safe — you won't be able to view the secret again after leaving the page. Under **User Authentication Settings**, add this redirect URI to your app: `https://Zernio.com/api/v1/connect/twitter/callback` Reach out to Dalek through [OshiHub](https://hub.oshi.social/) and provide your credentials. He'll help get your account configured in Zernio. You're all set! Start scheduling your posts. ## Need Help? If you encounter any issues during setup, reach out to Dalek for assistance through OshiHub.
